title: The RedeC2 Team
text:

  Scientific network

  
    
      The RedeC2 team brings together people working on long-term vegetation
      monitoring, floristics, functional traits, restoration, biodiversity
      synthesis and dry ecosystem conservation.

title: refloraR
text:
refloraR is an R package for accessing and analyzing plant specimen data from the REFLORA Virtual Herbarium, hosted by the Rio de Janeiro Botanical Garden. It provides tools for downloading, summarizing, and filtering herbarium records in Darwin Core Archive (DwC-A) format via the REFLORA IPT.


You can install the development version of refloraR from GitHub with:
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("DBOSlab/refloraR")
library(refloraR)



A general description of the available main functions (reflora_download and reflora_summary) that extract original REFLORA collections are provided below.


The following code can be used to extract a summary of all REFLORA-associated collections, including herbarium acronym, curator’s email contact, number of records and a direct link to the original REFLORA Integrated Publishing Toolkit (IPT).
library(refloraR)

summary_df <- reflora_summary(verbose = TRUE,
                              save = TRUE,
                              dir = "reflora_summary")
By specifying a vector of herbarium acronyms, the user can extract a summary for just the specific herbarium collection.
summary_some_df <- reflora_summary(herbarium = c("ALCB", "RB", "HUEFS", "US", "K"),
                                   verbose = TRUE,
                                   save = TRUE,
                                   dir = "reflora_summary")



The following code can be used to download original specimen records in DwC-A format and associated metada for all REFLORA collections.
library(refloraR)

reflora_download(verbose = TRUE,
                 dir = "reflora_download")
By specifying a vector of herbarium acronyms, the user can download specimens records for just the specific herbarium collection.
reflora_download(herbarium = c("ALCB", "HUEFS", "RB", "US", "K"),
                 verbose = TRUE,
                 dir = "reflora_download")




Full function documentation and articles are available at the refloraR website.



Cardoso, D. & Calderón, C. 2025. refloraR: An R Package for Exploring Plant Specimen Collections from REFLORA Virtual Herbarium. https://github.com/dboslab/refloraR

title:
Rede
        C2
text:
Research Network · Brazil

      
        Rede
        C2
      

      

      
        Permanent plots for understanding Caatinga and Cerrado dynamics
        across Brazil's seasonally dry ecosystems.
      

      
        Caatinga
        Cerrado
        Permanent plots
        Dry ecosystems
      

      
        Explore sites
        See protocols
      
    

    
      
      
      Comparable field data for conservation, restoration and synthesis.
    

  


  The RedeC2 network

  
    
      
        RedeC2 is a collaborative research network dedicated
        to understanding the multifunctional dynamics of Brazil's seasonally dry
        tropical ecosystems, especially Caatinga and Cerrado.
      

      
        Bringing together researchers from multiple institutions, the network
        integrates permanent plots, floristic inventories, functional traits and
        environmental data to investigate how plant communities are structured
        and how they respond to climatic seasonality, soil conditions and
        landscape context.
      

      
        Across different sites and regions, RedeC2 studies how natural gradients
        and human-driven disturbances influence vegetation structure,
        successional pathways and the spatial turnover of species.
      
    

    
      Overarching goal
      
        Generate robust, comparable data that support conservation planning,
        ecological restoration and evidence-based management of dry ecosystems
        in Brazil.
      

      
        Floristics
        Dynamics
        Restoration
        Open workflows
      
    
  



  
    
      
      Sites, data, and outputs
    
  

  

    
      
    

    
      Sites
      Permanent plots across Caatinga and Cerrado formations.
      01
    

    
      
    

    
      Gradients
      Seasonality, soils, structure and landscape context.
      02
    

    
      
    

    
      Products
      Comparable data for conservation, restoration and synthesis.
      03
    

  


  Integrated monitoring workflow

  
    
      01 · Permanent plots
      
        Repeated measurements of woody vegetation, herbs, regeneration and
        coarse woody debris in standardized units.
      
    

    
      02 · Species and traits
      
        Floristic inventories, taxonomic standardization and functional trait
        records connected to ecological gradients.
      
    

    
      03 · Environmental context
      
        Climate, soil and landscape layers combined with field records for
        cross-site comparability.
      
    

    
      04 · Synthesis
      
        Outputs for ecological theory, biodiversity conservation, restoration
        planning and public decision-making.
      
    
  





Participating institutions




Empresa Brasileira de Pesquisa Agropecuária (EMBRAPA)



Institut de Recherche pour le Développement (IRD)



Instituto de Pesquisas Jardim Botânico do Rio de Janeiro (JBRJ)



Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew



Serviço Florestal Brasileiro (SFB)



Universidade do Estado de Mato Grosso (UNEMAT – Cáceres)



Universidade Estadual Paulista (UNESP – Rio Claro)



Universidade Federal de Lavras (UFLA)



Universidade Federal de Minas Gerais (UFMG)



Universidade Federal de Pernambuco (UFPE)



Universidade Federal do Ceará (UFC)



Universidade Federal do Vale do São Francisco (UNIVASF)



Università di Torino (UniTO)



University of Exeter






  Contact

  
    
      Email
      c2redec2@gmail.com
    

    
      Repository
      
        
          github.com/C2rede

title: Protocols
text:
Field methods · Data standards · Partner manuals

      
        Protocols
        for C2
      

      

      
        Standardized methods keep field data comparable across sites, teams
        and years.
      

      
        Caatinga
        Cerrado
        Permanent plots
        Data standards
      
    

    
      
      
      Field methods, harmonized tables and partner manuals.
    

  



  Protocol logic

  
    
      
        Rede C2 works with permanent plots in dry ecosystems and connects
        with broader monitoring initiatives.
      

      
        This page gathers core protocol references that can support plot
        establishment, remeasurement, field standardization, data harmonization
        and cross-network comparability.
      
    

    
      Recommended anchor
      
        The DRYFLOR field manual is especially relevant because it offers a
        standardized workflow for permanent plots in seasonally dry tropical
        forests, including plot layout, tagging, remeasurement and woody stem
        monitoring.
      

      
        DRYFLOR DOI
      
    
  

  
    
      Plots
      Layout, tagging and remeasurement.
    

    
      Woody stems
      Diameter, recruits and mortality.
    

    
      Herbs
      Regeneration and herb layer modules.
    

    
      Modules
      Soils, traits and disturbance.
    
  


  Core protocol references

  
    
      Rede C2
      Network base
      
        Space for Rede C2’s own protocols, field sheets and coding standards
        for plot installation, recensus, disturbance records, traits and soils.
      
      
        Plot protocol
        Datasheets
        Field codes
      
    

    
      DRYFLOR field manual
      Dry forest permanent plots
      
        A key manual for establishing and remeasuring permanent plots in
        seasonally dry tropical forests. Useful for woody stems, tagging rules
        and long-term comparability.
      
      
        Protocols page
        Português
        English
      
    

    
      ForestPlots.net
      Field resources
      
        Field resources, publications and practical material that support
        tropical plot monitoring and interoperability across networks.
      
      
        No campo
        Publications
      
    

    
      SECO
      Dry tropics compatibility
      
        Protocol pages and modules aligned with DryFlor, ForestPlots and
        related networks for dry-tropics monitoring.
      
      
        Protocols
        Project
      
    

    
      SEOSAW
      Linked woodland modules
      
        Interoperable protocols and datasheets for woodland monitoring, useful
        as a reference for compatible modules and workflow design.
      
      
        Manuals
      
    

    
      Citation anchor
      Portuguese DRYFLOR manual
      
        Use the DOI as a stable citation anchor when referring to the Portuguese
        DRYFLOR field manual in Rede C2 methods and protocol references.
      
      
        DOI
      
    
  


  Suggested Rede C2 workflow

  
    
      01
      Plot establishment and remeasurement
      
        Plot geometry, subplot layout, coordinates, tagging, recensus intervals
        and replacement rules.
      
    

    
      02
      Woody stems census
      
        Diameter standards, multiple stems, damage codes, recruits, mortality
        and field note structure.
      
    

    
      03
      Herb layer and regeneration
      
        Modules for herbs, seedlings and regeneration tracking compatible with
        dry-forest and savanna workflows.
      
    

    
      04
      Soils, traits and disturbance
      
        Optional compatible modules inspired by partner networks to maximize
        interoperability and ecological interpretation.
      
    
  


  Datasheets and standards

  
    
      Datasheets and field sheets
      
        Minimum tables for recording plot metadata, stems, recruits, mortality,
        disturbance notes and observer information.
      
      
        Plot metadata
        Stems
        Observers
      
    

    
      Field codes and data standards
      
        Controlled vocabularies for species status, stem condition, damage,
        phenology, collection status and data-quality flags.
      
      
        Controlled vocabulary
        Quality flags
        Harmonization

title: Sites - Rede C2
text:
Permanent plots · Caatinga + Cerrado

      
        Sites
        & gradients
      

      

      
        Explore Rede C2 field sites and the environmental gradients connecting
        dry forests, savannas, soils and seasonality.
      

      
        Caatinga
        Cerrado
        Permanent plots
        Interactive map
      
    

    
      
      
      Spatial structure, plot metadata and environmental context.
    

  


  
    
    
    
    
    
    

    
    
    
    
    
    
  


  Interactive map

  
    Use the map to inspect Rede C2 permanent plots, vegetation classification,
    plot area, census number and climatic or institutional metadata available
    in the project table.
  






  
    Tip: keep the image carousel light by using optimized WebP/JPG versions
    and only load high-resolution images when necessary.
  


  How to read the network

  
    
      Spatial coverage
      
        Sites are distributed across formations and regions that capture
        ecological turnover in dry ecosystems.
      
    

    
      Environmental gradients
      
        Climate, soil, vegetation structure and landscape context should be
        interpreted jointly with plot-level data.
      
    

    
      Long-term comparability
      
        Repeated censuses make it possible to connect local dynamics with
        broad-scale ecological patterns.
